# Implementation progress ‚Äî the build backbone

**Owned by the Steward** (`agent/playbooks/federation/steward.md ¬ß2a`). This
file tracks execution **against the implementation DAG**
(`05-implementation-dag.md`), the build's analog of `spec/SPEC-PROGRESS.md`. It
**survives compaction**: on a cold start or after a compact, read this first,
then continue from the frontier (below). Update it **every synthesis pass and on
every WP state change**. The plan lives in `05`; this file tracks *progress
against it*. Run until complete, blocked, or instructed (¬ß2b).

**Status legend:** `not-ready` (deps unmet) ¬∑ `ready` (deps met, unassigned) ¬∑
`active` (a team is building) ¬∑ `in-review` (PR open / QA / CI) ¬∑ `merged`
(landed + retro in). Gates: `met` / `in-progress` / `not-started`.

>   **PX8-H** (Task #27) ‚Äî **‚úÖ‚úÖ MERGED `aab1f831` (PR #779) + all 3 ¬ß10 retros IN (leader `evt_3rfsyza35ngbd` / qa `evt_4ttx52kdkprj1` / impl `evt_7x48pgdqenr0v`; all three carry the CI-red exhaustive-consumer-ledger carry-forward) ‚Üí Task #27 CLOSED (2026-07-19 ~00:16Z).** _(historical detail below)_ **üîµ was ACTIVE (Runtime) on `wp/px8h-heterogeneous-continuation-composition` (origin tip `1acee25d`; local WIP checkpoint `9da7e0d3`).** Kicked `evt_21efmpkqak09s` after handoff-gate compaction. Route Architect ¬ß14 + Runtime QA (no CV). Owner Runtime, Size M, Risk High. Brief `docs/program/wp/PX8-H-heterogeneous-continuation-composition.md`. The seam = the missing PX7-O/P heterogeneous eliminator-frame case: an env-resolved `ComputationalRecursorClosure` feeds a known ordinary match (`after_read`) before returning to outer computational frames; the syntactic classifier can't see the env-resolved closure so the intervening ordinary continuation isn't installed ‚Üí dynamic `ReadProgress` match hits the fail-closed default. **FOUR sequential Architect design rulings converged the mechanism (full detail in decision log; each was Architect's lane ‚Äî Steward tracked + fixed transport, incl. 2 stranded-paste bare-Enter clears on the architect seat):** (1) **carrier = (B) caller-provenance** (`evt_h706fqp1dqa8` @ 11:50) ‚Äî thread the pending ordinary continuation from the reaching `lower_computational_producer_expr` `Let`, NOT by scanning the recursor's base body; (2) **peel exactly ONE layer** (`evt_13xfc1thegtxz` @ 12:24) ‚Äî the split is the outermost `ComputationalRecursorClosure` node vs its `recursive` payload (cardinality one; each IH mint wraps once); (3) **operational vector = `PendingLet ‚Üí head ‚Üí tail ‚Üí suffix`** (`evt_6nz2kfs9h6r8s` @ 12:40) ‚Äî `eliminators[0]` consumes the raw `HostResult` from `Effect FsReadAt`, which the ITree `Ret|Vis` head can't; PendingLet is the only correct first adapter (ordinary-lowers the response, invokes the residual base once ‚Üí produces the ITree value ‚Üí then head consumes); (4) **‚òÖ leaf-to-root shared decomposition at ALL THREE consumers** (`evt_6vmt0tmyhmb0m` @ 12:58, the resolving ruling) ‚Äî the stored recursor chain is a *continuation-nesting path, not an execution stack*: storage order is outer‚Üíinner, **execution order is inner‚Üíouter**. `after_read` is owned by the **terminal recursor node immediately above the residual base** (structural identity, NOT a default string / constructor name / chain length / body scan). ONE shared rule: walk `recursive` collecting each node's `{cases,default,outer_env}` in storage order ‚Üí first non-recursor = residual base ‚Üí **reverse ONLY that collected stored chain once** (inner‚Üíouter) ‚Üí apply identically at all three reaching paths (caller-provenance PendingLet; ordinary `lower_expr` recursor call; `lower_computational_producer_expr` recursor call). The reverse-only-at-Pending partial was an *incomplete application of the one invariant*, not a heuristic. Preserve each frame byte-for-byte + call-env `arguments‚Üícaptures‚Üísaved/producer_env`; `lower_dynamic_constructor_match`/HostResult matching/defaults/final-kind/PX7-O¬∑P negatives all unchanged; not a new prerequisite. Discriminator: 3-layer distinct-per-layer-defaults must store outer‚Üímiddle‚Üíinner but execute inner‚Üímiddle‚Üíouter; 1-layer unchanged; reverting the shared decomposition to storage order recovers the exact wrong outer default; exercise all three consumers (rejects the reverse-only-at-Pending partial). **Progress: H-P6 materially advanced** ‚Äî at checkpoint `9da7e0d3` the checked source compiles/links/runs and observes `FsOpen√ó2, BufferAllocate, FsReadAt(ReadSome 6), ResourceRelease√ó3` (was hitting the type contradiction; now only the `after_read`/ReadProgress consumer selection remained ‚Üí ruling 4 closes it); still 0 `FsWriteAt` + `RuntimeTrap(4)` until the shared decomposition lands at all three consumers. Implementer built ruling 4 (all three consumers, checkpoint `afeba2fc`) ‚Äî **the ReadProgress‚ÜíResourceBracketResult mismatch is now RESOLVED.** **‚è∏ FIFTH HARD-STOP (~13:04, `evt_2w6d6jqg6pkwg`, under ruling-4's "if a failure remains" clause):** H-P6 now fails LATER at object emission ‚Äî an ordinary **5-layer** recursor call over-consumes through the final exit layer, passing `ProcessExitStatus` into a surrounding ordinary `Result::{Err,Ok}` match ("ordinary Match got ProcessExitStatus; cases=[Result::Err,Result::Ok]"); consumer log = ordinary recursor-call decomposition with stored chain lengths 1,3,7,then 5. Implementer's read: "execute the whole stored chain at every ordinary recursor-call consumer over-consumes layers that belong outside that ordinary call's source context" (leaf‚Üíroot correct for the ReadProgress seam, over-broad here). Hard-stopped with exact value-kind/cases rather than filtering by length/default/body or weakening the Result match. Architect's lane ‚Äî reached cleanly. **‚úÖ ARCHITECT RULED (6th) @ 13:27 (`evt_5p7ef4yvmcaac`, mentions implementer): the 4th hard-stop is valid ‚Äî keep leaf-to-root, but do NOT execute an inherited outer continuation twice across the caller-provenance `Let`; the missing op is exact RECURSOR-LINEAGE OVERLAP CANCELLATION. Still PX8-H (no new prereq).** Root cause (Architect re-reproduced on `afeba2fc`, 10 linear commits off `fe6e5360`): the caller-provenance `Let` owns a COMPOSITION of two chains with an inherited common outer suffix ‚Äî producer `[A‚ÇÄ,A‚ÇÅ,B‚ÇÄ,B‚ÇÅ,C‚ÇÄ,C‚ÇÅ,D]` and body continuation `[B‚ÇÄ,B‚ÇÅ,C‚ÇÄ,C‚ÇÅ,D]`; the current impl runs the outer suffix, then Pending, then the SAME outer suffix again (`ProcessExitStatus` into the `Result::{Err,Ok}` match). Fix (8-step): (1) give every minted `ComputationalRecursorClosure` layer a compiler-private clone-stable `RecursorFrameProvenance` identity (private to `Lowered` ‚Äî NO Runtime IR/kernel/ABI/wire/host/surface change; mint fresh at the 2 node-construction sites, cloning retains inherited identity); (2) shared decomposition returns residual + frames leaf-to-root retaining provenance; (3) when the reaching `Let` has BOTH a direct env-resolved recursor-call value AND an admitted body direct call to an env-resolved recursor continuation, decompose BOTH chains before lowering either; (4) compute maximal common outer suffix BY PROVENANCE IDENTITY (not length/case/default/body/env/hash); (5) split producer=`producer_unique_inner++shared_outer`, body=`body_unique_inner++shared_outer`; (6) invoke producer residual once under `producer_unique_inner ‚Üí PendingLet(body residual) ‚Üí body_unique_inner ‚Üí shared_outer ‚Üí existing caller suffix` (7/5 carrier ‚Üí `A‚ÇÄ‚ÜíA‚ÇÅ‚ÜíPendingLet‚ÜíB‚ÇÄ‚ÜíB‚ÇÅ‚ÜíC‚ÇÄ‚ÜíC‚ÇÅ‚ÜíD`); (7) PendingLet binds `[value]+saved_producer_env`, invokes body residual once, call-env `arguments‚Üícaptures‚Üísaved_producer_env`, no re-resolve; (8) raw-host-value case (no chain) stays `PendingLet‚Üíbody chain inner‚Üíouter‚Üísuffix`, standalone ordinary recursor call stays full leaf-to-root (generic arm not authorized to guess a cut). Corrects the prior "execute whole stored chain at every consumer" wording: every OWNED chain still runs leaf-to-root, but the caller-provenance `Let` owns two chains ‚Üí quotient the shared suffix once. H-P4 strengthened (3-frame-shared-suffix distinct defaults; byte-near chains w/ different provenance can't cancel; empty/exact overlap controls; suffix-before-Pending mutation recovers the ProcessExitStatus refusal; raw-Effect + standalone controls). Implementer built ruling 6 (provenance IDs + shared decomposition + suffix comparison), compiles at clean `fbe35572`. **‚è∏ SEVENTH HARD-STOP (~13:36, `evt_4y4xyxcbfwm8s`, mentions architect+leader):** the authorized direct-Call/direct-Call `Let` quotient is a ZERO-HIT seam ‚Äî overlay counters show 0 hits for both `lower_computational_producer_expr::Let` and `lower_expr::Let` paired-chain overlap. The real compile path is exclusively the standalone ordinary `lower_expr::Call` arm (layers 1,3,7,then 5), then the surrounding ordinary `Match{scrutinee: Call Var}` gets `ProcessExitStatus` vs `Result::{Err,Ok}` ‚Äî i.e. the 5-layer call is emitted while lowering a selected continuation INSIDE the 7-layer call, and the caller-owned pending Result consumer sits at the ordinary `Match(Call Var)` boundary, NOT a syntactic `Let`. (This is the ORIGINAL fixed defect shape: env-resolved recursor feeding a known ordinary match.) Provenance/decomposition/suffix machinery is in production but no cancellation fires because the ruled `Let` admission shape is absent. Implementer refuses to widen `Let‚ÜíMatch` or infer the pending-frame threading without an explicit correction. **Architect's lane. Reached cleanly (mentions architect); Architect ENGAGED (ran `git show fbe35572:cranelift_backend.rs`) ‚Äî BUT its seat was BLOCKED ~15min on a safety-check interstitial modal defaulting to "Retry with a faster model" (T1 downgrade). ‚úÖ Steward cleared it selecting "Keep waiting" ‚Üí architect back Working on `gpt-5.6-sol high` (T1 preserved), ruling the Let‚ÜíMatch widening.** Implementer idle-waiting. **Awaiting the 7th ruling ‚Üí implementer resumes ‚Üí candidate SHA.** Mechanism = Architect's call (not Steward); Steward = transport/infra + tracking only. _(Op note for Pat's check-in: PX8-H is a genuinely thorny native-lowering composition ‚Äî 7 hard-stops deep, but PROGRESSING each round (failure moves strictly deeper, checkpoints advance), not stalled; no operator decision needed, it's mechanism. New infra hazard handled: a Codex safety-check modal can block a T1 seat mid-ruling and defaults to a model downgrade ‚Äî Steward must select "Keep waiting" to preserve T1 for hard rulings.)_ **‚ñ∂ ON candidate SHA:** verify base/scope ‚Üí credentialed push ‚Üí one Decision (verify OBJECT resolved) ‚Üí Architect ¬ß14 + Runtime QA ‚Üí publisher non-doc-only ‚Üí verify byte-identical + PIN ‚Üí doc-only sync ‚Üí chase retros ‚Üí then unblock PX8-F.

Native arbitrary-precision `Int` lowering** ‚Äî completes native `Int` at the value-representation + primitive-lowering layer so dynamic arithmetic (the `add_int file_offset count` writeAll boundary that PX8-H stops at) computes exact mathematical Z, not wrapping i64. Contract (Architect): `Native Int = Small exact i64 | Big canonical content-addressed integer`; add/sub/mul operate exactly, Small fast-path only when the exact result fits, promote before overflow, canonicalize back to Small iff it fits; eq/leq exact across Small/Big; host narrowing = checked range conversion to the host's unsigned width (out-of-range ‚Üí the op's `InvalidOffset`/`InvalidBounds`, never truncation/wrap). NO kernel rule / public surface op / trusted primitive / writeAll intrinsic / range-postulate authorized. >   **PX8-J** (Task #29, NEW ‚Äî Architect ruling `evt_2gs0zfg66x9qk` @03:00 07-19) ‚Äî **‚õî not-ready (Steward FRAMES shovel-ready NEXT; Runtime-owned). Continuation terminal-answer-boundary closure.** Exposed by the PX8-I overlay: `merge_scalar_branch`'s process-object fallback (`cranelift_backend.rs:4402-4471`) mints `ProcessExitStatus`/`ExitCode` while a computational eliminator is still live ‚Üí `ComputationalMatch: scrutinee is not a constructor value`. **Fixed scope (Architect):** `ProcessExitStatus` minted ONLY at a proven terminal-answer boundary ‚Äî no ordinary/computational/active/source continuation can still consume the value; at every scalar join use the **preplanned checked answer-kind cut**; aggregate `Constructor` values stay in **branch-local prefix P** until their eliminators drain. > **(B) NEW WP `PX8-N` ‚Äî Runtime-owned checked-host BOUNDED-NAT reply-lowering compiler prereq (`evt_22y0emyj6cwf2`, thr_2epbr82x2nnfx).** This is the fix for PX8-F's hard-stop; **its own Runtime compiler WP** (soundness/discriminators independent from the PX8-X train ‚Äî do NOT bury it in the train; own QA + ¬ß14). Mechanism: a compact semantic **`BoundedNat`** lowering carrier minted ONLY while reifying a validated host reply ‚Äî NOT a public `Int->Nat` primitive, NOT an eager unary chain. At the response boundary validate op/tag + full progress invariant BEFORE minting (count>0, count within effective request, ReadSome span length=count, start+length checked within admitted buffer, ReadEof the only zero-read, zero-write stays `NoProgress`); reify the private `BufferSpan` budget as `BoundedNat(count)` + `TransferCount` projections from the SAME validated scalar (one mint ‚Üí span length/transfer count/attempt budget can't diverge). Carrier implements `Zero|Suc predecessor` elimination + computational recursion over a strictly-decreasing predecessor, retaining exact IH + binder env (general checked-host result mechanism, NOT a `writeAll` intrinsic). May stay one machine scalar internally but every source-level observation MUST equal structural `Nat` semantics; NO caller-selected fuel, NO `Int` budget field, NO construction outside the private checked-result path. **Admit + fully lower the 4 ops** `BufferAllocate`/`BufferFreeze`/`FsReadAt`/`FsWriteAt` (exact request fields, success ctors, all existing structured resource/IO errors ‚Äî flipping availability without constructing the checked result is non-conforming). Host-effect ABI bytes may stay stable (existing tag-dependent `HostReplyV1` scalar layout sufficient; NO ABI expansion). WP must include: (1) resource-independent bounded-Nat unit cases (zero/succ, multi-step recursion, exact predecessor/IH order, malformed/over-bound reject); (2) response-boundary positives (ReadEof, short ReadSome, short Wrote) + negatives (zero/over-bound); (3) the real linked checked PX8-F minimized fixture as **evidence-only overlay** (native `writeAll` recursion reaches + agrees with interp); (4) a discriminator that breaks decrement / substitutes the raw scalar and fails the fixture.

> > _(ruling as fixed inputs, still authoritative below:)_
> ## _(prior header retained:)_ ‚úÖ CAP-MODEL RULING DELIVERED + catalog reshaped
>
> > **‚úÖ ARCHITECT CAP-MODEL RULING DELIVERED ‚Äî `evt_7k8n8rwj1xbh1` (thread `thr_szhcns1f2mpe`).** Comprehensive +
> > shovel-ready. Acked mention-free (`evt_26wr6qdwtw3cm`); catalog reshaped (`03-program-of-work.md` rows + star
> > notes) with the ruling pinned as fixed inputs. **THE RULING (frame these verbatim as fixed inputs):**
> > ‚Ä¢ **META = NEW ADR** (capability-evolution/process-admission; NOT an ADR-0018 amend; rides as **PX13's lead
> >   deliverable**; ADR-0017 stays honesty boundary, ADR-0018 stays native-effect/ABI/differential contract).
> > ‚Ä¢ **PX13 = `chmod` ONLY** ‚Äî distinct `RightSet::CHANGE_MODE` + `FsCapabilityOperation::ChangeMode` (WRITE/METADATA
> >   don't imply; AFull yes / APartial no; attenuation intersects); `fchmod` on the authorized **no-follow** handle,
> >   NO path re-lookup, accept only `mode & !0o7777 == 0`. Observe **mode not owner**: node obs gets `mode:
> >   Option<u16>` = `st_mode & 0o7777` (Some regular/dir, None symlink/other); NO uid/gid/ts/acl/xattr/inode/dev. The
> >   catalog/registry/observer/consumer closure + manifest hash + wire layout + **PX6 twin-root delta all move
> >   together**; append ONE FS op ID, fail closed on old inventory/hash. **chown/chgrp REJECTED** (‚Üí future
> >   `CHANGE_OWNER` WP). Negative controls: WRITE-w/o-ChangeMode denies pre-leaf; ChangeMode-w/o-WRITE succeeds;
> >   attenuation drops ChangeMode; symlink/path-swap can't redirect the handle; mode-mutation changes delta, uid/gid
> >   drift alone doesn't.
> > ‚Ä¢ **PX14 = effective-root admission, NOT a cap token** ‚Äî v1 predicate exactly `geteuid()==0` via the audited
> >   ken-host rustix boundary (real/saved/fs-uid + caps(7) + userns + securebits DEFERRED). Surface = header marker
> >   `program capabilities FS <authority>, RootExecution Allow` (omission=deny); NOT a `ProgramCaps a` field, not an
> >   FS right, not a forgeable scalar, **CLI/env may NOT add it** (compiler binds into native plan/hash). ONE shared
> >   startup-admission fn (immutable euid snapshot + declaration) called by BOTH executors **before** ProcessContext,
> >   **before** any cap-table grant, **before** first effect; posture witness records admission+SIGPIPE done.
> >   Refuse-root = `TerminalErrorV1::RootExecutionDenied` (unit variant, startup terminal: empty
> >   trace/delta/stdout/stderr, exit via shared `ProcessExitCode->i32`); native init writes that canonical terminal
> >   obs with NO live ProcessContext. Controls: non-root proceeds / euid0-no-marker exact-denies + zero host leaves /
> >   euid0-with-marker proceeds / CLI-env-plan mutation can't manufacture the marker / both executors share the pure
> >   checker; test injection at host-observer seam ONLY.
> > ‚Ä¢ **PX15 = `./` (cwd) ONLY, ready now** ‚Äî typed root-spec variant, capture cwd ONCE at cap-table init, open handle
> >   then, no ambient cwd dep after; suffix component-by-component under `ScopeEscape`/`SymlinkDenied`; obs relative to
> >   resolved cap root (never cwd spelling).
> > ‚Ä¢ **`~/` (home) ‚Üí SPLIT to new PX16, ‚õî NEEDS-OPERATOR** ‚Äî `$HOME` REJECTED (forgeable); `getpwuid_r(geteuid())`
> >   is **libc/NSS policy, NOT a Linux syscall** ‚Üí crosses the operator-settled **rustix/linux_raw-only** seam (PX1).
> >   PX16 owns the NSS boundary (trusted surface + dep delta + bounded failure + startup snapshot + injectable diff
> >   seam), then `EffectiveUserHome` enters the enum. **Escalated to Pat** (supply-chain/dependency-posture call, sib
> >   of the "whose unsafe" fork; I will NOT sanction the libc/NSS TCB expansion unilaterally). Logged window-log #18.
> >
> > **NEXT (I initiate ‚Äî NOT event-gated):** (1) **author 3 shovel-ready frames** `docs/program/wp/PX13-*.md`,
> > `PX14-*.md`, `PX15-cwd-*.md` (¬ß2c: ruling's choices as FIXED INPUTS w/ do-not-reopen guard, mandated deliverable
> > outline, testable ACs incl. each ruling's negative controls, ADR-0017 CI-green-not-local-workspace AC wording; the
> > new ADR is PX13's lead deliverable). (2) **handoff-gate the Runtime ring** ‚Äî runtime-leader `agt_37reqrd72cg00` +
> > runtime-implementer `agt_37reqg3nync00` + runtime-qa `agt_37reqvb6ce400` (all home-clean/idle, PX5C retros in;
> > compact ALL THREE unconditionally via `scripts/handoff-gate-compact.sh runtime-leader runtime-implementer
> > runtime-qa` in bg, verify drops WIDE, handle post-compact rouse). (3) **release PX13 FIRST** (carries the shared
> > ADR the others cite; Runtime is ONE ring ‚Üí PX14 + PX15(`./`) wait as READY, pulled after PX13). **PX7 HELD**
> > (Resource/Bracket ‚Äî do NOT release/re-ask). Pat offline until ~11:30 UTC; window log =
> > docs/program/autonomous-window-log-2026-07-16.md (entries 17-18).
> >

> **NEW WPs from operator (Pat, 2026-07-16) ‚Äî capability/privilege surface (DESIGN SESSION to resume):**
> - **PX13 CREATED** (03-program-of-work.md) ‚Äî FS mode/ownership ops: add `chmod`, eval `chown`/`chgrp`
>   as a versioned `HostOpV1` catalog extension (ADR-0018 ¬ß1). NOT-READY: deps PX5+PX6 merged + Architect
>   cap-model ruling on 3 gates (new mutation right-bit per ADR-0017; `FsDeltaV1` extend to observe
>   mode/uid/gid ‚Äî currently ignored; chown/chgrp-need-privilege vs chmod-defensible). grep-confirmed
>   chmod/chown absent from all crates/+spec/ today. Detailed frame authored at release time (¬ß2c), not now.
> - **PX14 CREATED** (Pat confirmed "add a WP for that", 2026-07-16) ‚Äî root-execution posture capability.
>   A `ProgramCaps`-declared allowance that lets a program **already started as root** (euid 0) *continue*
>   at root; absent it, a program that finds itself root at fail-closed startup **exits with a stable
>   terminal error before any effect**. A cap can't *escalate* (caps attenuate; launcher/OS sets euid) ‚Äî
>   this only permits continuing. NOT-READY: deps **PX5 merged** (rides the same fail-closed startup-posture
>   seam as the SIGPIPE ruling `dec_1gk5vbw2bbg05` ‚Äî euid check at ProcessContext init, before first host op)
>   + shared Architect cap-model ruling. Ruling gates: privilege predicate (euid==0 vs richer uid-triple +
>   capabilities(7)); stable error id (`RootExecutionDenied`); ProgramCaps surface. Both executors identical.
>   Runtime-trusted (ADR-0017), no kernel rule, no linear types.
> - **PX15 CREATED** (from Pat's FS-cap path-root requirement, 2026-07-16) ‚Äî FS capability **path-root
>   grammar**. A `ProgramCaps` FS authority root may be `~/‚Ä¶` (home of executing **euid**, resolved @ startup)
>   or `./‚Ä¶` (**cwd @ execution start**), not only absolute. Resolves to the ADR-0018 ¬ß2 **root handle** ONCE
>   at cap-table init, identically in both executors, snapshotted @ startup (per-op re-resolution would break
>   ¬ß4 relative-path canonicalization + PX6 twin-root differential). NOT-READY: deps PX5 merged + cap-model
>   ruling. Ruling gate: home source = `getpwuid(euid)` (recommended: principled, non-ambient) vs `$HOME`
>   (ambient/forgeable). Scope/symlink checks apply relative to resolved root regardless of spelling.
> - **ANSWERED Pat's ADR question:** ADR-0018 does NOT restrict the surface *spelling* of the cap path root
>   ‚Äî it fixes only the *resolved runtime* form (root handle ¬ß2; scope/symlink checks; ScopeEscape/
>   SymlinkDenied; ¬ß4 relative-raw-byte-path canonicalization ignoring absolute root names). `~/`,`./`,abs
>   all legal as long as they resolve to ONE fixed root handle at init, identically across both executors.
> - **SHARED Architect cap-model ruling** gates PX13+PX14+PX15 (one ruling, 3 axes). Amend-ADR-0018-vs-new-ADR
>   is the Architect's call. All 3 hold ADR-0017 honesty (runtime-trusted + discriminator-tested, no proof).
> - **MULTI-ROOT FS AUTHORITY ‚Äî CONSIDERED & DECLINED by Pat (2026-07-16). DO NOT re-raise.** Pat asked
>   whether two FS roots (e.g. `~/` + `/tmp`) would be two caps or one cap with a path-list. Answered:
>   ADR-0018 forces **two separate `Cap` values** (one root handle each; ops take exactly one cap; ambient
>   path‚Üícap lookup forbidden) ‚Äî never a path-list inside one cap; and it's not expressible today anyway
>   (spec `33-declarations.md:183`: "at most one authority per effect family"; `ProgramCaps a = MkProgramCaps
>   (Cap a)` is authority-monomorphic). Offered a PX16 (multi-root/heterogeneous FS authority) ‚Üí **Pat: "no,
>   nvm, ADR-0018 is better as is."** Keep the single-authority-per-family model; NO PX16. Settled input.

> named audited `abi_v1` unsafe module (`deny` not `forbid`), no host-unsafe into
> runtime/lowering/interp/CLI; SIGPIPE‚Üí`IOError::BrokenPipe`. (2) IR gains
> `Effect{family,operation:HostOpV1,capability:Option<RuntimeCapabilityUse{identity,value}>,
> args}`; live cap = opaque generational `{slot,generation}` token into a host-owned
> table from declared `ProgramCaps`; cap-check has ONE impl below both executors (NO

> BOTH TargetAbi + effect hashes embedded; one immutable append-only response arena for
> the WHOLE entry invocation. (4) canonical `EffectObservationV1{stdout,stderr,
> fs_delta,terminal_error,effect_trace,exit_status}`, one ordered event per Vis; error
> identity is an ENUM (exact cap denials + IOError incl Other(raw_os_code) where already
> observable); one shared error-map fn both lanes; launcher-owned out-of-band trace sink.
> (5) **PX6 ‚Üí new `crates/ken-verify` (Verify-owned)**; twin byte-identical real temp
> roots (interp PosixHost@A vs native artifact@B); CaptureHost = negative/unit only.

> **Console.Write/Flush/IsTerminal + FS.WriteFile/ReadFile** (5 of 14) ‚Äî minimal but
> exercises the WHOLE contract (ambient + capability route + stdout/stderr + FS-delta +
> response-arena dynamic Bytes + cap/FileError identity + trace + exit). Other 9 stay
> named unavailable lanes.

> `cranelift_backend.rs:1371-95` Bytes=compile-time `Vec<u8>`, Constructor=CT tag;
> `:1454-64` Match only for statically-known ctors; `:1422-36` rejects runtime Bool
> branch; `:1931-57` bytes_length/at need CT Bytes/static index; `:1272-85` binds
> only a scalar ABI load + optional CT-staged RuntimeValue. So consuming OS-variable
> `List Bytes`/`List (Prod Bytes Bytes)` (ProcessInput) needs EITHER a new runtime
> aggregate/control-flow/lifetime model OR a new process-field-access primitive
> family ‚Äî **both new backend mechanisms beyond PX4's wiring scope.**

> **‚òÖ One coherent principle behind all four (PRINCIPLES #9 Ken/Ward split):**
> Ken stays the static/propositional/total core; **behavioral & resource-safety
> machinery is pushed OUT to the runtime + Ward; research-grade type theory
> (affine/linear, deeper OTT) is explicitly DECLINED in the language.** Resource
> lifetime and testing are both *obligations sent to Ward*, not built into Ken.

> **Four operator rulings, recorded in charter ¬ß2 + ¬ß4a + ¬ß5-toolchain
> (`09-posix-linux-abi-campaign.md`, committed steward/work; publish to main with
> the fossil-frame doc-only bundle):**
> 1. **`rustix` APPROVED for the runtime trust boundary.** FORK 2 dependency-risk
>    acceptance GRANTED. `ken-kernel` stays `forbid(unsafe_code)`. **PX1 fully

>    at PX7's frame (charter ¬ß4a decision-point row rewritten). Linear/affine +
>    further OTT extensions are open research ‚Üí OUT of the language.

>    exactly-once release operationally (generation-checked handle table; close at
>    the runtime level, not Ken types); the *assurance obligation* is routed to
>    **Ward** via the assumption-boundary export (ADR-0006, G-Ward-seam). Ken
>    reports runtime-enforced / `tested`, never `proved`. This is PRINCIPLES #9

>    now: wire resource-lifetime assurance into the Ward seam** (was: re-open R2).
> 4. **The in-Ken test framework is TEMPORARY SCAFFOLDING until Ward takes over.**

> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE ARCHITECT'S AX-2 BLOCK ‚Äî *"preserving `len`/`contains`/iteration is NOT signature compatibility"*
> The candidate changed **`GlobalEnv::trusted_base(&self) -> Vec<GlobalId>`** ‚Äî a
> signature **pinned normative/stable by `spec/10-kernel/18 ¬ß4`** ‚Äî into a new
> `TrustedBase` type. It kept `len`/`contains`/owned-ID iteration, **so every
> existing assertion still passed.** But downstream type annotations and ordinary
> `Vec` ops break; `TrustedBase` equality now includes **labels**; and its owned
> iteration has a different element view from `entries()`.
> **Required close (no AX-1 reopening):** retain `trusted_base() -> Vec<GlobalId>`
> exactly, and read each label through the already-required `Decl::Opaque.name` via
> `env.lookup(id)` ‚Äî **which is precisely the landed ¬ß18 contract.** Any convenience
> projection must be **additive under a distinct name**, never an implicit
> replacement of a frozen method.
>
> **‚òÖ AND QA APPROVED THIS ‚Äî the lesson is why.** QA verified **behavioral**
> compatibility (*"historical ID iteration/contains/len semantics remain
> available"*) and that is **TRUE**. The spec pins the **SIGNATURE**. **A frozen API
> is frozen at its TYPE, not at its behavior** ‚Äî and a green suite cannot see the
> difference, because every caller in-repo was migrated in the same diff. **This is
> the expressibility/frozen-surface family: the break is in the TYPE surface, where
> no value-level test looks.** My own AX-1 frame said *"`trusted_base()` must become
> readable **without churning existing count assertions**"* ‚Äî the implementer
> satisfied **my assertion-level wording** and broke **the spec's type-level pin.**
> **My wording invited it.**
>
> ### ‚òÖ MY D3 GATE ‚Äî QA's evidence says it is MET (verify by CONTENT at merge, not on their word)
> `ElabCtx.owner_label: String` **non-optional** ¬∑ both standalone APIs require a
> caller-supplied `Into<String>` ¬∑ **no ownerless overload** ¬∑ `Decl::Opaque { name:
> String }` + positional `declare_postulate(env, name: String, ‚Ä¶)` ¬∑ **and
> `declare_postulate_raw` requires `name: &str`** (the back door I had not named ‚Äî
> QA closed it) ¬∑ kernel reads confined to `trusted_base()` audit projection
> (`TrustedBaseEntry { id, name }`), **zero** reads in conversion/typing/admission/
> positivity/universe/elimination ¬∑ 3 formatter oracles green.
> **‚áí At the merge Decision: re-verify these BY CONTENT on the diff. Adds
> `conformance/**` ‚áí ‚õî FULL CI, NEVER `--doc-only`.**
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ LET-5 ‚Äî the frame's two additions beyond the Architect's ruling
> 1. **Binding count is a RED HERRING and the frame forbids building to it.** A
>    one-binding `let` and a group's outer node have the **same resolved form**.
>    Grouped bodies passed because they were *inferable applications*; singletons
>    failed because they put a *checked-mode `match`* behind the `let`. **‚õî no
>    `bindings.len()==1` special case.**
> 2. **THE FALLTHROUGH IS A CLASS.** `check()` spans 474..646 and has arms for only
>    **6 of 16** `RExpr` variants (`RApp/RCon/RLam/RMatch/RNumLit/RStr`); the `_ =>`
>    arm dumps the rest to `infer`+`unify`. That is **correct and lossless for
>    INFERABLE variants** (`RVar/RUniv/RAsc/RArrow/RPi/RProj/RBinOp`) and **wrong
>    only for GOAL-PROPAGATING ones**: **`RLet`** (confirmed) and **`ROld`** ‚Äî a
>    transparent wrapper whose subterm should inherit the goal, **structurally the
>    same leak, UNVERIFIED.** AC5 makes the ring settle `ROld` +
>    `RAttachedProofRef`: fix by the same rule, or record why the goal cannot matter.
>

> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ LET-5 ‚Äî the Architect's ruling (`evt_5dhcdfsvnvebe`). MY HYPOTHESIS WAS WRONG.
> **It is NOT a sort-from-binder bug and NOT a single-binding bug.** It is a
> **bidirectional-elaboration MODE LEAK**:
> - `check()` (`elab.rs:474`) has **NO `RExpr::RLet` arm** ‚áí a `let` in **checking**
>   position falls through to `infer()` and **loses the expected goal**.
> - `infer_match` then builds a constant `‚Ä¶ ‚Üí Type ‚Ñì` motive and **falls back to
>   `Type 0`** when the return classifier is `Œ©` ‚áí the kernel *honestly* reports
>   `expected: Type 0, found: Œ©0`. **The diagnostic is a downstream witness, not the
>   cause.**
> - **A one-binding `let` and a group's outer node have the SAME resolved form**
>   (`resolve.rs:1404`). **Binding count is IRRELEVANT.**
>
> **The repair (one rule, kernel + `infer_match` untouched):**
> `Œì ‚ä¢ rhs ‚áê A ; Œì,x:A ‚ä¢ body ‚áê weaken(expected,1) ‚üπ Œì ‚ä¢ let x:A = rhs in body ‚áê expected`
> Unannotated ‚áí infer RHS first. Emit the same `Term::Let`. **Do NOT special-case
> `bindings.len()==1`.** AC: pre-fix-red/post-fix-green on (a) Foundation's Œ©-match
> shape, (b) a singleton let around `Refl`; keep a wrong-RHS rejection; **re-run
> Foundation's exact harness as the integration witness**; zero kernel/grammar/
> formatter/catalog delta.
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ AND MY COVERAGE MATRIX WAS ON THE WRONG AXIS (fixed: `cbaae5e7`, PR #691)
> I published "single vs grouped √ó data vs proof" ‚Äî **binding count separated pass
> from fail PERFECTLY on the sample, and was a total red herring.** The real axis is
> the **let body's ELABORATION MODE** (inferable vs checked): grouped bodies passed
> because they were *inferable applications*; singletons failed because they put a
> *checked-mode `match`* behind the `let`.
> **‚áí Draw a coverage matrix's AXES on what the MECHANISM forks on, not on what the
> SYNTAX makes salient. An axis that perfectly separates pass/fail on a small sample
> is CORRELATION ‚Äî make someone name the code path before trusting it.**
>

> **The fork:** `ElabEnv::elaborate_expr` can check `Axiom : Top` **outside any
> declaration** ‚Üí no owner label derivable. **My D3 pin ‚Äî *"every `Axiom` sits
> inside some named declaration, so the label is ALWAYS derivable"* ‚Äî is FALSE.**
> (5th wrong population claim of the day: I enumerated *declaration-position
> producers* and never enumerated the **API entry points**.)
> **I ruled (B) reject. Architect ruled (A) require-a-caller-supplied-label. (A) WINS:**
> 1. **(B) makes a term's legality depend on WHICH API you called** ‚Äî an
>    entrypoint-dependent carve-out of a *universal* rule. Non-compositional; rots.
> 2. **My "caller-supplied label = fabrication" objection was wrong on its own
>    terms** ‚Äî a declaration name is *also* just an unvalidatable string. **The
>    invariant AX-1 buys is "the COMPILER never invents it"**, and a **required
>    parameter** delivers exactly that: a missing owner is a **Rust arity error**,
>    not a `None` branch. No Option, no default, no ownerless overload.
> 3. **At an API boundary the CALLER IS THE HOME** (REPL / test fixture). Provenance,
>    not fabrication.
> 4. **The Architect found a sentinel I'd have shipped** ‚Äî an `"instance resolution"`
>    `ElabCtx` on the branch. That's the tell their grounding was deeper.

> declaration **OR is supplied by the caller of a public standalone-expression API**;
> never inferred from the expression; ownerless call **unrepresentable**.
> **`32` UNCHANGED** (`Axiom` legal wherever a term is checked). **AX-2 does not merge

> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE FRAME/BUILD BRANCH COLLISION ‚Äî fixed in the playbook (`6aaf8c33`, PR #688)
> **I blocked my own ring.** I published the LET-3 P2 frame from
> `wp/let3-p2-map-acc-lookup`, it squash-merged as `2739c30c`, **my worktree
> stayed parked on it**, and `foundation-leader` physically could not check the
> branch out. It **held the kickoff and asked** rather than forking or resetting ‚Äî
> the right escalation. Root cause was in **my playbook**: step 1 said author the
> frame ON `wp/<ID>-<slug>`, step 3 squash-merges it, step 4 said the team
> *"continues"* it ‚Äî **impossible after a squash-merge** (remote deleted; local ref
> dangles AHEAD of main with its content already in).
> **‚áí TWO NAMES, ALWAYS: `wp/<ID>-frame` (mine, merges and dies) ¬∑
> `wp/<ID>-<slug>` (theirs, cut fresh from `origin/main`). And switch OFF the
> frame branch the moment you publish it.** AX-2 escaped this **only by luck**
> (I happened to name its branches `-frame` / `-build`). *A rule that only works
> when you happen to do what it never told you to do is a latent bug with good
> luck.* Fleet memory: `a-squash-merged-branch-cannot-be-continued.md`.
>

> was holding **Language's AX-2 design escalation**. A live block on the critical
> path, cleared automatically, zero diagnosis cost. **This is the whole argument
> for converting a discipline into a check.** Run the sweep FIRST, every tick.

> ### ‚òÖ LET-3 PHASE 2 ‚Äî what I framed, and the one rule that matters
> **Scope is a PROOF FAMILY, not a file.** The pilot frame said "family by
> family" then named `Map.ken.md` ‚Äî which is not a scope (15,352 lines, 403
> decls) and invites the very sweep the pilot existed to prevent. **Enumerated
> the real population: the `*_from_list_acc_lookup_*` family ‚Äî 34 lemmas, 3,442
> lines, 22% of the file**, in four structurally parallel sub-families
> (delete 11 ¬∑ union 5 ¬∑ intersection 9 ¬∑ difference 9). Exemplar confirmed:
> `union_from_list_acc_lookup_assoc_hit` (`:10218`, **237** lines ‚Äî the pilot
> frame said 238/13√ó; the true repeats are `insert_with_fold_step` **14√ó**,
> `lookup ‚Ä¶ acc` 9√ó, `assoc ‚Ä¶ xs2` 5√ó).
>
> **The deliverable is a SHARED VOCABULARY, not 34 nicer proofs.** The four
> sub-families ARE the same proof with four dispatches. 34 locally-improved
> proofs that each invented their own names = **FAILED**, gates green or not.
>
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ THE PARALLELISM LICENSE IS BOUNDED ‚Äî and it is my own door.** The pilot
> review let a **one-use** binding stand in a `True ‚Ü¶` arm *solely* for parallel
> structure with a `False ‚Ü¶` arm that had earned its names. At 34 lemmas √ó 4
> parallel families, that reasoning would justify **every binding anywhere** ‚Äî
> the pilot's own "a blind sweep answers yes everywhere" failure, arriving
> through the one door I left open. **So: a name must be EARNED by repetition or
> semantic weight in ‚â•1 member; once earned it may be MIRRORED into a sibling
> one-use position. Parallelism PROPAGATES an earned name ‚Äî it can never
> ORIGINATE one.** The tell: a binding justified by *"the other three have it"*
> whose chain never terminates in a member that earned it. **Delete the whole set.**
>
> **Oracles GROUNDED, not guessed** (the corpus-oracle audit ‚Äî this is the exact
> thing that has surfaced as red CI *after* review): `kenfmt_c_capstone.rs` and
> `kenfmt_b4_splicing.rs` **both glob `catalog/`** ‚áí both read `Map.ken.md`.

> wrong in the other direction. `map_build_acceptance.rs` already asserts
> `trusted_base_delta` ‚áí AC2 has a landed home.

> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ AX-1 SCOPE CHECKPOINT ‚Äî **MY OWN FRAME'S D3 WAS UNDER-SPECIFIED. RULING POSTED ‚Äî IT IS A FIXED INPUT.**
>
> **The mechanism I under-read:** the `Axiom` intercept lives inside the
> **generic `check()`** (`elab.rs:474`, arm at `:502`) ‚Äî **not** a
> declaration-body special case. So `Axiom` fires in **ANY checking-mode term
> position**, and **one declaration can mint SEVERAL postulates**
> (`lemma foo : T = f Axiom Axiom`). My frame enumerated a closed *corpus*
> population (two shapes ‚Äî true) and silently promoted it to a closed
> **mechanism** (false). **The same error as the morning's: I enumerated from the
> symptom, not from the gate.**
>
> **‚õî RULING (`evt_7ga79nfpafk87`; spec-leader relayed it as `evt_4egxmzz3sv3e9`):**
> **THE NAME IS A LABEL, NOT A KEY. The spec MUST NOT require uniqueness.**
> 1. **Name = the enclosing declaration path.** Top-level decl ‚Üí its own name;
>    instance field ‚Üí `Class.HeadType.field`. **ALWAYS derivable** (every term
>    position sits inside a named declaration) ‚áí no gensym, no "unnameable" case,
>    **no fabricated placeholder.**
> 2. **Non-uniqueness is LEGAL and HONEST.** Two postulates from one declaration
>    share a label, keep distinct `GlobalId`s. A positional disambiguator would be
>    **a lie dressed as precision ‚Äî KTR-2's exact bug, one layer up again.**
> 3. **¬ß3.4's coherence argument is DEMOTED to an observation** (why today's two
>    shapes *are* unique), **never a normative invariant.**
> 4. **Narrows nothing** ‚Äî `Axiom` stays legal in every term position; D5 intact.
>
> **‚òÖ AND THE AC THAT MUST LAND (the whole point of the change):**
> `Opaque { name: String }` ‚Äî **NOT `Option<String>`** ‚Äî and
> `declare_postulate(env, name: String, ‚Ä¶)` with the name a **required
> parameter**. **If it is optional or defaulted, "every postulate is named" lives
> in CONVENTION, and a producer that skips it is INVISIBLE IN A GREEN DIFF** ‚Äî
> the missing thing was never a value, it was *a place to write a guarantee*.
> **The required field/param makes the compiler force every producer** (surface
> `Axiom`, `bytes.rs`, `conversions.rs`, `capabilities.rs`) **to supply one.**
>

> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ AX-1 ‚Äî PAT APPROVED: **"sugar plus named postulates‚Ä¶ adding a label to kernel data is low risk. approved."**
> Frame: `docs/program/wp/ax1-axiom-sugar-and-named-postulates.md`.
> **Grounded facts (I read every link ‚Äî do NOT re-derive from memory):**
> - **`Axiom` has NO kernel support because no `Axiom` term exists.** Not in the
>   grammar, not the prelude, **zero hits in `ken-kernel/src`.** It is a
>   string-matched elaborator intercept (`resolve.rs:648` `SUGAR_AXIOM` ‚Üí
>   `elab.rs:508`) that calls `declare_postulate` to mint a **fresh `Decl::Opaque`
>   of the *expected* type.** The kernel sees an ordinary typed constant.
> - **‚öÝ THE TRUST BASE IS ANONYMOUS *AND WIDER THAN THE SURFACE*.**
>   `Decl::Opaque` has **no name field**; `declare_postulate` uses `fresh_id()`.
>   **`is_prelude` excludes EXACTLY THREE ids (`Top`/`Bottom`/`tt`) ‚Äî it is NOT a
>   prelude filter.** ‚áí **Rust-minted postulates are ALREADY COUNTED, anonymously**
>   (`bytes.rs:136/260/275`, `conversions.rs:133`, `capabilities.rs:428/432/444`).
>   **I first guessed these were prelude-excluded. WRONG ‚Äî in the reassuring
>   direction.** ‚áí **the name goes on `declare_postulate`, not on the sugar.**
> - **Surface population is CLOSED, 2 shapes only:** top-level decl body, and
>   instance field. Instance name = `Class.HeadType.field`. **Uniqueness is FREE
>   from coherence** (one instance per class+head) ‚áí **NO positional index** (it
>   would churn every DS `trusted_base_delta` assertion).
> - **‚õî KERNEL BAR: the name is a LABEL the kernel must NEVER read.** No branch on
>   it in conv/typing/admission/positivity/universes/elim. That is *why* it's safe.
>
> ### ‚öñ LET-3 CALIBRATION ‚Äî binding on Phase 2
> **`False ‚Ü¶` earns its bindings** (`sort_bool t` occurred **twice**). **`True ‚Ü¶`
> is the boundary** ‚Äî one-use bindings; accepted ONLY for cross-arm parallel
> structure. **‚õî NOT generalizable: a one-use binding does not earn its keep by
> consistency alone.** *(Foundation's implementer reached the same carry itself.)*
>

> ### ‚öñ ARCHITECT RULED LANE 2 (`evt_28bw30t80dx7d`) ‚Äî **no enclave gate needed**
>
> He **concurred** and independently re-derived my chain. **Settled, do not
> reopen:** no universe **inference** (a declaration's type would depend on its
> own body); no universe slot for the legacy **sugar** (it is intentionally the
> bounded `Type 0` non-indexed form); the **kernel gate stays authoritative** ‚Äî
> the *elaborator* translates the error; the kernel gets no surface advice.
>
> **He also CLOSED the question I flagged as unverified:** file elaboration is
> **in declaration order**, so a **forward** reference must reject exactly like
> an undefined name ‚Äî while an **earlier** declaration must keep working.
>
> ### ‚õî KTR-2's core: a PRODUCTION sort-fabrication bug
>
> **`data.rs:391` fabricates `Term::ty(Level::Zero)` for an unknown type name**
> ‚Äî the *same* sort-as-placeholder idiom KTR-1 was built to catch in fixtures,
> sitting in the elaborator itself. Legacy sugar (`:64`) uses the **unchecked**
> `rtype_to_kernel`; the explicit form uses `rtype_to_kernel_checked` (`:433`),
> which correctly returns `UnresolvedCon`. **Reachable**, because `resolve_type`
> (`resolve.rs:1589`) never rejects an unknown type name (`TCon ‚Üí RCon`
> unconditionally; `TVar` *falls back* to `RCon`).
>
> ‚áí `data D = C Foo` (Foo undefined) **silently declared `C : Type 0 ‚Üí D`** ‚Äî
> pre-KTR-1 a typo produced a wrong declaration with **no error at all**. KTR-1
> converts it from **silent** to **loud-but-misleading** (a *universe* error for
> an *undefined name*). **Strict improvement; KTR-2 makes it honest.**
>
> **‚õî DO NOT mass-replace `Term::ty(Level::Zero)`** ‚Äî most sites are CORRECT
> (`Œî_p` params `:46`, genuine `Type` lowering `:417`/`:490`). Only the
> **unknown-name fallback** dies. *The idiom is right in one position and wrong
> in another.*
>

> ### üî¥ THE ARCHITECT SEAT WAS WEDGED ‚Äî how I found it, so you find it faster
>
> Both rings went QA-green and then **nothing moved for 5 minutes.** The
> Architect pane was **idle at an empty prompt with BOTH terminal-review
> requests sitting UNSUBMITTED in its input buffer as pasted content.** Its
> last turn ended at 19:17; the two mentions (KTR-1 19:23, LET-2b 19:25)
> arrived as pastes and **never got an `Enter`.**
>
> **`post_response` returning an event id proves the event EXISTS, not that any
> agent READ it.** A single `tmux send-keys -t moot-architect Enter` submitted
> both and it woke immediately. **‚áí On any "ring is green but the gate is
> silent" stall, `capture-pane` the GATE seat FIRST and look for an unsubmitted
> paste.** (Already in fleet memory as a convo-mention hazard ‚Äî this is its
> second sighting, now on the Architect.)
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ LANE 2 RE-GROUNDED ‚Äî AND IT IS NOT THE FORK I ANNOUNCED
>
> I told the space Lane 2 was a fork: *"does Ken INFER the family universe, or
> REQUIRE an annotation?"* **I read the whole chain on `origin/main` and the
> annotation escape ALREADY EXISTS, fully plumbed.** Verified link by link:
>
> | link | file | what it does |
> |---|---|---|
> | surface AST | `ast.rs:596` | `TUniv(Option<u32>, Span)` ‚Äî `Type 1` is real |
> | resolve | `resolve.rs:516` | `TUniv(level,_) => (indices=[], level)` |
> | elaborate | `data.rs:161` | `level.map(level_from_nat).unwrap_or(Zero)` |
> | kernel | ‚Äî | `InductiveSpec { level: Succ(Zero) }` |
>
> **‚áí `data D : Type 1 where { C : (s : Type) ‚Üí D }` declares the family at
> level 1; KTR-1's `Œî‚Çñ` check sees arg-type `Type 1 ‚â§ 1` and ADMITS it. Today.
> No new machinery.**
>
> **What is ACTUALLY broken is narrower ‚Äî and I nearly handed the enclave an
> invented fork instead of it:**
>
> 1. **The SUGAR form cannot lift.** `data D a = C A | ‚Ä¶` (grammar `32:33`)
>    has **no family slot at all**, and `data.rs:79` hardcodes `level: Zero`.
>    No escape *in that form*.
> 2. **The diagnostic is a raw `ConstructorUniverseViolation`** ‚Äî it never tells
>    the author "annotate the family as `: Type 1`". **This is the real
>    deliverable.**
>
> **My recommendation (to the Architect, NOT an open fork):** **do not infer,
> and do not grow the sugar a slot.** Inference would make a declaration's
> **type depend on its own body** ‚Äî adding a constructor could silently lift
> `D`'s universe and break every downstream user at a distance. That is exactly
> what predicativity and explicit annotation protect. The explicit form is
> already the documented general form (`32-grammar:121`: the sugar *is* sugar
> for a non-indexed family). **‚áí Lane 2 collapses to a DIAGNOSTIC WP + one spec
> sentence, not an enclave elaboration.** Route it to the **Architect** (he is
> already in KTR-1 context); do **not** spin up an enclave Handoff Gate for it
> until he rules otherwise.
>

> 1. **"Green-vs-green makes a standalone WP unverifiable" is WRONG.** B2W-1
>    disproves it (overlay against the held gate). The real distinction is

> 2. **‚òÖ The carriers are NOT "sitting right there."** I said `Bool`/`Nat`/`Unit`

> ### üîë LANE 2 ‚Äî GROUNDED AT THE PRODUCER (2026-07-14, by me, verified)
> **`data.rs` never infers the family universe:**
> - **legacy family `:79` ‚Äî `level: Level::Zero` HARDCODED**; params all `Type 0` (`:46`).
> - **explicit family `:158` ‚Äî `level.map(level_from_nat).unwrap_or(Level::Zero)`.**
> - surface `Type` ‚Üí `Term::ty(Level::Zero)` at `:417`, `:490`.
> - **‚òÖ THE GRAMMAR IS THE REAL CONSTRAINT** (`spec/30-surface/32-grammar.md`):
>   `:34` explicit form HAS a `data_family` slot ‚áí **`data D : Type 1 where ‚Ä¶` IS
>   expressible.** `:33` sugar form `data D a = C A | ‚Ä¶` **has NO family slot at
>   all** ‚áí **legacy CANNOT express a lifted family. No escape, no inference.**
>
> **‚áí The fork for the enclave/Pat:** does Ken **INFER** the family universe from
> its constructor args, or **REQUIRE** an annotation? If "require," the sugar form
> **cannot say it** ‚Äî so does it grow a slot, or get deprecated? And today the
> author gets a **raw `ConstructorUniverseViolation`** ‚Äî a diagnostic gap either way.
>
> **‚öÝ UNVERIFIED, DO NOT REPEAT AS FACT:** `data.rs:391` lowers an **unknown name**
> to `Term::ty(Level::Zero)` ‚Äî a silent type-level placeholder that would now trip
> the same gate. **Probe reachability before framing it.**

> ## ‚úÖ LET-4 MERGED ‚Äî `26527c5a`, FULL CI green, content-verified
> `LetBinding` in `parser.rs`; `let4_multi_binding` present. **AWAITING 3 RETROS**
> (chased `evt_16spbds40b1g1`). **Merged ‚âÝ closed ‚Äî I cannot run LET-2b's Handoff

>
> **The lesson (for LET-2b's QA plan):** the first candidate `935681e0` was
> **QA-APPROVED and DEFECTIVE.** QA tested comments ‚úì and shadowing ‚úì ‚Äî **never
> their COMBINATION with a distinct prefix** (its shadow cases put the shadow at
> index 0, where there is no prefix to move, so the buggy backward-scan had nothing
> to re-collect and looked correct). **Both axes green, product red. A matrix that
> walks each axis separately is not a matrix ‚Äî it is two lines.** Architect found it
> with **ONE focused diagnostic**, not a broad rerun.

> ## ‚ñ∂‚ñ∂ KTR-1 RELEASED TO KERNEL ‚Äî trust-root repair (`docs/program/wp/ktr1-constructor-universe-gate.md`)
> **The gap is CONFIRMED and it is a CONFORMANCE DEFECT, not an open question.**
> The Architect executed the decoder too (`evt_66d1p9bz1h621`): `data D : Type
> where { C : (s : Type) ‚Üí D }` **plus** `fn decode (d : D) : Type = match d { C s
> ‚Ü¶ s }` ‚Äî **both exit 0.** That is a same-level Tarski universe: `D : Type 0`
> injecting every `Type 0` (including `D`), with `decode (C A) ‚â° A`. **Nobody has
> derived `‚ä•` and we do not claim one** ‚Äî but the checker is outside its
> predicative contract, and that is enough.
>
> **`spec/10-kernel/14-inductive.md` ALREADY MANDATES THE GATE** (verified at the
> producer): *"constructor argument types must live at `‚Ñì` or below"*; admission
> requires *"(c) universe-level checks."* And ¬ß3: *"Predicativity keeps this sound;
> **there is no special restriction beyond the universe-level checks.**"* **‚áí The
> spec's whole soundness argument for large elimination RESTS on the check that was
> never implemented.** `check.rs:945`'s comment ‚Äî *"positivity is the sole
> structural admission gate"* ‚Äî **is the bug, written down.**
> **Fix formation, NOT elimination. No `Prop`. No design fork.**
>
> **‚òÖ THE TRAP I PUT IN THE FRAME IN CAPITALS:** `Œî_p` (params) ‚âÝ `Œî_k`
> (constructor-local args). Sweep params into the level walk and you compute
> `level(Type u) = suc u > u` and **reject `List`, `Maybe`, `Vec` ‚Äî everything.**
> That failure reads as *"the gate is wrong"* and invites weakening it. It isn't.
> **AC4 also warns: grepping `data` in `.ken` sources MISSES the Rust-emitted
> prelude** ‚Äî enumerate BOTH, count at the producer.

> `ken check` **exit 0** on `origin/main @ 26e9baed`. (He also verified the legacy
> `=` spelling was *parser*-rejected ‚Äî i.e. he checked his negative wasn't a false
> negative before trusting his positive. That clause is why I believe the result.)

> `check.rs:918-966` `declare_inductive` enforces **ONLY** strict positivity
> (`:945` ‚Äî *"the sole structural admission gate"*) + `synth_type`. **There is NO
> check that constructor ARGUMENT SORTS fit under the declared family level.**
> **The gate I could not find is NOT THERE.**
>
> **‚öÝ WHAT IS *NOT* ESTABLISHED ‚Äî hold this line:** admitting a large inductive is
> the **PRECONDITION** for Girard's paradox, **not the paradox**. The open question
> is whether **ELIMINATION is unrestricted** over such a family. **Nobody has
> derived `‚ä•`.** Do not let anyone (me included) round this up.
>
> **Why it's serious anyway: there is NO `Prop`.** Coq admits large inductives too
> and contains them with an impredicative sort + **singleton elimination**. Ken has
> neither ‚Äî `sort_pi`/`sort_sigma` = `max` (`check.rs:183-202`), `Type ‚Ñì : Type
> (suc ‚Ñì)`, `Omega` predicative + level-indexed. **So there is no containment story
> already in the design for this to be leaning on.** Either one exists undocumented,
> or the gate is missing.
>
> **STATE: with the @architect for adjudication (posture vs trust-root gap; and
> containment if a gap). Kernel ring HELD ‚Äî remediation NOT started, by my
> direction:** `declare_inductive` is a **TCB admission gate**, the one place a
> well-intentioned patch can cost more than the bug.
> **I SEQUENCED THE ARCHITECT: LET-4 terminal review FIRST** (bounded, zero kernel
> delta, gates four WPs ‚Äî minutes), **THEN this with full attention.** *A confirmed
> gap doesn't worsen in 30 min; it does worsen if adjudicated in a hurry.*

> ## ‚öñ‚öñ OPERATOR RULINGS ‚Äî RESOURCE MANAGEMENT + THE RESIDUAL-GAP LEDGER (Pat, 2026-07-14)
>
> ### 1 ¬∑ Ken's surface will NOT claim resource safety ‚Äî and will not pretend to
> **No clear theory unifies affine types with OTT** (QTT/Idris-2 has the quantities
> and no observational equality; cubical/observational systems have the equality and
> no substructural layer; **nobody has both**). ‚áí **Linear/affine tracking is NOT on
> the roadmap** (*"not a research project"*). **DESIGN:** opaque **generation-checked
> handles** + a **sealed bracket**, **enforced in the RUNTIME**; residual emitted
> honestly across the Ward seam, **never as `Q`.**
> **The export's kernel-side discriminator makes over-claiming STRUCTURALLY
> IMPOSSIBLE** (`71-assumption-boundary.md:118-127`): a claim is `Q` **iff** its
> certificate `check`s **and** its goal is not a postulate in `trusted_base()`.
> *The honesty is mechanical, not a discipline we maintain by vigilance.*
> ‚áí `ken-cli-program-i-contract.md` CORRECTED (`ac55eefc`): streaming handles are
> **NOT** gated on linearity. That gate was **a prerequisite that is never coming** ‚Äî
> a permanent silent veto.
>
> ### 2 ¬∑ Ward stays SPEC-ONLY until Ken matures ‚Äî but do NOT design around that
> **Pat, verbatim: *"it's not a question of whether ward is spec only. we are
> building for the future where ward and ken are complete."*** **‚áí Ownership
> boundaries are decided on the MATURE END-STATE. A scheduling fact must never be
> baked into an architectural boundary.** *(I answered "Ken owns the ledger because
> Ken is the only thing that runs." **That was a scheduling answer to an
> architecture question, and Pat rejected it. Correctly.**)*
>
> ### 3 ¬∑ ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE RESIDUAL-GAP LEDGER IS **TWO** ARTIFACTS, NOT ONE
> **The ruling principle: OWNERSHIP FOLLOWS EPISTEMIC ACCESS. Each side owns the
> gap it is the ONLY party that can SEE.** *(Not convenience. Not schedule.)*
>
> | gap | question | owner | why ONLY they can see it | status |
> |---|---|---|---|---|
> | **STATEMENT** | *what did Ken never SAY?* | **KEN** | **absence from the export is INVISIBLE to a consumer.** Ward cannot enumerate what it was never handed. | ‚õî **DOES NOT EXIST ‚Äî this is the work** |
> | **DISCHARGE** | *how PARTIAL is the evidence?* | **WARD** | only the discharger knows its engines' limits ‚Äî depth `k`, sampling coverage, monitor window, abstraction gap | ‚úÖ **RATIFIED** ‚Äî `bound` + 4-way outcome (`ward 12-discharge-attestation.md:96-102`) |
> | **THE JOIN** | *is the total assurance SUFFICIENT?* | **GOVERNANCE POLICY GATE** (ken ¬ß64/¬ß65) | the only place both halves are visible | Ward's spec already says it: *"the gate reads the outcomes, the policy decides sufficiency"* |
>
> > ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ AND THE CLAUSE THAT SETTLES THE SEQUENCING
> > **The JOIN CANNOT CATCH THE DANGEROUS GAP.** The policy gate iterates over
> > obligations that **EXIST**. **A property nobody ever stated HAS NO ROW.** ‚áí **No
> > downstream consumer ‚Äî however complete Ward becomes ‚Äî can EVER detect it.**
> >
> > **‚áí Ken's statement-gap gate cannot be deferred to "when Ward is ready," and the
> > reason is NOT that Ward is unbuilt. It is that WARD COULD NEVER DO IT.**
> > *The one gap invisible to every consumer is the one the PRODUCER must gate at
> > emission.*
>
> **‚òÖ WHAT MAKES IT A GATE AND NOT A WISH-LIST ‚Äî `Œ£` IS CLOSED BY CONSTRUCTION.**
> `Œ£` is not a list someone maintains; **it IS the denotation's interaction-tree
> perform-node signatures.** ‚áí **GATE: every symbol in `Œ£` must be either (a) carried
> by ‚â•1 `T`/`P` obligation, or (b) EXPLICITLY declared un-stated, with a reason.
> Build fails otherwise.** *You cannot silently fail to state a property, because the
> alphabet is closed and the gate demands coverage of every symbol.*
> **This is `add_decl`'s bypass-grep, applied to obligations** ‚Äî the same closure
> lesson that cost me the AC4 inventory today. *(See
> `an-enumeration-needs-a-proven-closure-not-a-better-grep`.)*
>
> ### 4 ¬∑ THE RESOURCE WP PATH (framed, not yet released ‚Äî LET/KTR chain first)
> **RES-0** B2W-1 witness repair *(RELEASED)* ‚Üí **RES-1** runtime floor (opaque
> gen-checked handle + sealed bracket + structural `close`; **design against the
> NATIVE backend from day one** ‚Äî the interp's `Rc` store will accept a design that
> cannot lower to Cranelift) ‚Üí **RES-2** the **closure gate** *(build the guard
> FIRST ‚Äî same logic as PX6-before-PX7)* ‚Üí **RES-3** state the `T` obligations
> (`no use-after-close`; `every open is eventually closed`), **forced complete by
> RES-2.**
> ### üî¥üî¥ PROBED ‚Äî AND I WAS WRONG. **`Œ£` IS TOO COARSE TO STATE THE OBLIGATIONS.**
> **I told Pat: *"`Œ£` is literally the interaction-tree perform-node signatures, so
> `open`/`read`/`close` ARE perform nodes ‚áí 'no use-after-close' IS expressible as a
> `Temporal Œ£` formula."* **THAT IS FALSE.** *(Probed 2026-07-14 before framing
> RES-1 ‚Äî which is the only reason it is not now a three-WP-deep discovery.)*
>
> **What is TRUE (verified at the emission, not the doc):**
> - **FS ops ARE effects** ‚Äî capability-gated, dispatched through `run_io`'s effect
>   path (`eval.rs:4362` ‚Üí `fs_dispatch:4046`, `args.get(1)` = the capability;
>   unknown ops ‚Üí `RunIoError::UnknownEffect`). **‚úÖ RES-1 does NOT have to invent
>   perform nodes. That risk is dead.**
> - **BUT `Œ£` = `EffectRow::effects()` = a set of `EffectName`** (`export.rs:307-310`;
>   `row.rs:65`), and **`EffectName = String`**, documented `row.rs:14` as *"A named
>   effect: **`FS`**, `Clock`, `Console`, `Net`, `Rand`."*
>
> **‚áí `Œ£` TODAY = `{"FS", "Console", ‚Ä¶}` ‚Äî ONE SYMBOL FOR ALL OF FS.**
> **NOT `{fs.open, fs.read, fs.close}`.**
>
> **‚áí `G(close(h) ‚Üí ¬¨F use(h))` IS NOT EXPRESSIBLE.** It needs `close` and `use` as
> **distinct alphabet symbols.** *The property has nowhere to be said.*
>
> > **‚òÖ HOW I GOT IT WRONG ‚Äî `grep-the-emission-not-the-name`, my OWN memory, 3rd
> > time today.** `export.rs:29` and `:163` both say ***"Œ£ = L5 perform-node
> > signatures."*** **Three lines below, `:310` collects EFFECT NAMES.** **The doc
> > comment is aspirational; the emission is coarse.** I trusted a survey that
> > trusted the comment. **A doc comment is a CLAIM about the code, not the code.**
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ AND IT BREAKS THE CLOSURE GATE TOO ‚Äî this is the load-bearing consequence
> My proposed gate ‚Äî *"every symbol in `Œ£` is covered by an obligation or explicitly
> declared un-stated"* ‚Äî **is VACUOUSLY SATISFIABLE at effect-name granularity:**
> `"FS"` appears in *some* obligation ‚áí **gate passes, and you have said NOTHING
> about `close`.** **The gate only has teeth at OPERATION granularity.**
>
> **‚áí BOTH halves of the resource plan ‚Äî stating the obligations AND the gate that
> forces them complete ‚Äî depend on the SAME missing thing:**
>
> > ## ‚ñ∂ **RES-Œ£ (NEW, and it is the TRUE FIRST WP of the campaign): refine `Œ£` from
> > ## effect NAMES to operation SIGNATURES.**
> > Touches `EffectRow`/`EffectName`, the export emitter's alphabet derivation, and
> > **invariant I3 (alphabet closure)**. **It sits AHEAD of the runtime floor, not
> > behind it.** *Build the alphabet you must speak in, before you build the thing
> > you must describe.* **Same shape as the native-early and PX6-before-PX7 rulings:
> > if you design against the coarse alphabet you will design something you cannot
> > say ‚Äî and find out only after the semantics are public.**
>
> **‚öÝ REMAINING UNVERIFIED PREREQ ‚Äî probe before framing, do NOT pin:** (a) **`emit_export`
> has NO production call sites** (only `b1`/`b2`/`b3` acceptance tests + its own
> definition) ‚Äî nothing in the CLI emits an export today; (b) **I have NOT confirmed
> FS ops flow through the ITree `Vis`/perform-node path** rather than direct host
> calls. FS effect names DO exist (`fs`, `fs_cap`, `fs_resp`, `file`, `io_read`), but
> *names ‚âÝ perform nodes.* **If FS ops are not perform nodes, RES-1 must MAKE them
> so, and that is much bigger than "add a handle."**
>
> ## ‚òÖ‚òÖ RESOURCE DESIGN ‚Äî the scope-indexed report (`local/ken-scope-indexed-resource-middle-path-report.md`)
> **It CORRECTS ME, and it is right:** a phantom `Handle scope File` is NOT enough
> ‚Äî **index the OPERATION'S EFFECT** (`Eff (FileOps scope) A`), and let **only the
> bracket discharge it.** The guarantee lives in **the ABSENCE OF A RUNNER for an
> escaped thread**, not in a field on the reference. *That is the real runST lesson;
> I had it half-right.*
>
> **BUT ITS MECHANISM DOES NOT PARSE, and that breaks its most important gate.**
> Its attack is `(scope : Type 0) √ó Handle scope File`. **Ken has NO SURFACE Œ£** ‚Äî
> kernel has it (`term.rs:294`), surface does not: no Œ£ syntax, no pair literal,
> no `.1`/`.2`; **`√ó` is LEXED AND NEVER PARSED** (only the formatter consumes the
> token); prelude `Pair` is **non-dependent, `Type 0`-only.** ‚áí **Probe D.3
> (existential escape) ‚Äî which the report itself calls "the most important
> discriminator" ‚Äî is specified against SYNTAX THAT DOES NOT EXIST and would
> PASS TRIVIALLY FOR THE WRONG REASON.** **A vacuity trap in the one gate that
> matters.** **RE-SPECIFY IT AGAINST GADT EXISTENTIALS** (`data ‚Ä¶ where { Pack :
> (s : Type) -> Handle s File -> ‚Ä¶ }` ‚Äî these DO parse, `parser.rs:1094-1177`).
> ***Third time this month: conclusion TRUE, reason WRONG.***
>
> **AND THE MIDDLE PATH IS NOT CHEAP ‚Äî I was optimistic.** It needs three things
> that DO NOT EXIST: **(1) type-indexed effects** ‚Äî an effect is *literally a
> `String`* (`EffectName = String`; rows are name-sets), so `Eff (FileOps scope) A`
> is a **real effect-system feature, not a near-miss**; **(2) a scoped/delimited
> handler** ‚Äî none exists, handlers are **tail-resumptive folds** and
> non-tail-resumptive is a **HARD elaboration error** (`36-effects.md:1187`), which
> is exactly the bracket shape; **(3) type-indexed capabilities** ‚Äî `Cap` carries an
> `EffectName` string. Also **`opaque` is NOT a keyword** (use abstract export,
> `33-declarations.md ¬ß4.2`).
>
> ### ‚áí THREE LAYERS. The floor is MANDATORY regardless of the fork.
> **(1) RUNTIME FLOOR ‚Äî build it; it is NOT scaffolding.** Opaque
> generation-checked handles + sealed bracket. **‚òÖ v1 EXPOSES NO `close` AT ALL ‚Äî
> the bracket owns release.** That does not *fail* to enforce exactly-once; **it
> REMOVES THE OBLIGATION** ‚Äî which is how *"safest possible"* and *"not a research
> project"* stop fighting. **(2) THIS UNBLOCKS STREAMING NOW:**
> `ken-cli-program-i-contract.md ¬ß2.3` says **verbatim** *"do not add streaming file
> handles until affine/linear resource tracking exists"* ‚Äî **TOO STRONG; correct
> it.** **(3) The middle path = a real FEASIBILITY WP** with the probe re-specified,
> **gating the API freeze** (once the first resource package ships, files/sockets/
> processes/timers/epoll all depend on it; retrofit is brutal). **R2/linear stays
> research.**

>
> ## ‚úÖ‚úÖ ALL THREE CAMPAIGN FORKS RULED BY PAT. THE POSIX CAMPAIGN IS FULLY COMMITTED.
>
> > ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ‚òÖ PAT'S INTENT ‚Äî the sentence every PX frame is measured against
> > **"The intention is to make Ken a software engineering language. Not a toy,
> > not a research project, but a tool for doing real practical work in the safest
> > way possible."** *(2026-07-14)*
>
> **RULINGS:** **(1) scope = COMMIT THROUGH PX-E** (boundary ‚Üí native effects ‚Üí
> resources ‚Üí processes/sockets ‚Üí event loop). **(2) `rustix` ACCEPTED** in the
> **runtime** trust boundary ‚Äî `ken-kernel` untouched, keeps `forbid(unsafe_code)`.
> **PX1 is UNBLOCKED.** **(3) NATIVE EARLY** ‚Äî **PX-B runs BEFORE PX-C.**
>
> **Campaign exit:** *a real CLI tool **and** a real network service, as **native
> binaries**, scoped-capability-confined, over files larger than memory,
> observationally identical to interpreter runs ‚Äî **and the dangerous logic
> (parsers, path policy, protocol state machines, attenuation) PROVED, not merely
> tested.*** **A verified `cat` is a toy; a proved path-confinement layer over a
> small audited syscall boundary is the pitch.**
>
> **Order:** `PX0`(merging) ‚Üí LET-4 ‚Üí LET-2b ‚Üí LET-3 ‚Üí CC9 ‚Üí **PX1 ‚Üí PX2 ‚Üí
> PX3/PX4 ‚Üí PX5 ‚Üí PX6 ‚Üí PX7 ‚Üí PX8 ‚Üí PX9 ‚Üí PX10/PX11 ‚Üí PX12.**
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ TWO THINGS I OWE, BOOKED AS NAMED OBLIGATIONS ‚Äî NOT vague research items
> **(a) The R2 fork RE-OPENS AT PX7's FRAME.** *"Through PX-E"* + *"safest way
> possible"* **collide at exactly one place.** PX-D/PX-E are a **resource
> explosion** (sockets, epoll, timers, pipes, children, mappings) ‚Äî and **Ken
> cannot state "exactly-once release."** No affine types. Runtime *enforces* it;
> the **type system cannot express it**, and **no test shows the gap** (tests
> exercise VALUES; the hole is in the TYPE SURFACE). Today it's harmless ‚Äî **there
> is no `open`/`close` at all.** **PX7 INTRODUCES the hazard.** Tie-break: **ship
> it, enforce in runtime, report `tested` NEVER `proved`, disclosure IN THE
> SOURCE.** ‚õî **Do not smuggle affinity into PX7** (Pat: *"not a research
> project"*). **Decide at the seam ‚Äî retrofitting affinity onto a public,
> catalog-depended resource API is far worse.**
> **(b) THE TOOLCHAIN AXIS ‚Äî verified against `main`, and PX buys NONE of it.**
> Diagnostics ‚úÖ (CC4). Modules ‚öÝ **`export` is SPECIFIED AND NOT PARSED** (zero
> hits in `parser.rs`). Test/property framework ‚õî **nothing** (no
> `catalog/packages/Test`; CC9 unbuilt). Package manager ‚õî not started.
> **‚áí "Not a toy" is NOT purchased by syscalls alone ‚Äî a language you cannot write
> a test in is not a tool for real practical work.** **I bring a separate
> toolchain proposal; it is NOT smuggled into PX.**
>
> ### ‚úÖ‚úÖ PX0 MERGED + **CLOSED** ‚Äî `origin/main @ f729c67e` ¬∑ CI green ¬∑ retros IN
> **Runtime is FREE.** Next for Runtime is **PX1 ‚Äî waits on CC9 ‚Üí LET chain. Not
> released.** **‚òÖ Fleet law changed on PX0's retros (`d323b2e3`):** the memory
> `a-red-base-gate-is-not-your-bug` **ITSELF set a trap** ‚Äî it guards *over-repair*
> and thereby invited *over-disclaim*. The implementer reproduced the red hunks on
> the parent, concluded "inherited," **and was wrong** (his own assertion added
> one). **"The base is red" ‚áè "my delta is clean."** Fix = QA's **two-ref
> ownership oracle**: `check(candidate) \ check(parent)` = YOURS (block these
> only); `‚à©` = inherited (route, don't touch); **normalize line drift first** or
> you re-attribute an inherited hunk to yourself and land back in over-repair from
> the other side.
> **Content-verified on `main`:** 54 `target_os = "linux"` gates; the only
> surviving `cfg(unix)` pair is `os_string_bytes` (2324/2330); all **13 ABI facts
> present, VALUES UNCHANGED** (we did not "fix" what we had not probed); **zero
> kernel/`Cargo.lock` delta.** `main` no longer claims a confinement guarantee it
> cannot deliver. **‚è≥ Chasing the three Runtime ¬ß10 retros ‚Äî merged ‚âÝ closed.**
>
> **‚òÖ‚òÖ MY ERROR, AND WHAT CAUGHT IT.** I pinned *"3 constants, 5 FFI decls"* in
> the frame. **It was 11 and 6** ‚Äî off 3.6√ó, **in a document whose thesis is "the
> artifact cannot state its own contract."** *I indicted the thing I failed to
> enumerate.* **Caught THREE times** ‚Äî Architect, leader's D0, QA's independent
> count at the producer. **Not by a test. Not by a gate. By three people who
> counted instead of trusting me.** Standing law in every PX frame now: a handoff
> says ***"there are N; here are all N"*** ‚Äî **N counted, never inherited from a
> citation.** *A line number tells you where something IS, never where its KIND
> begins and ends: I read from `:2370` because an audit cited `AT_REMOVEDIR` at
> `:2375`; the inventory began at `:2355`.*
>
> **‚òÖ The leader's D0 also caught a scope error I authored:** my frame said
> *"re-gate every `cfg(unix)` carrying a Linux ABI value"* ‚Äî but `os_string_bytes`
> is `cfg(unix)` and **portability-only, NOT an ABI carrier.** Re-gating it would
> have broken non-Linux builds for an unrelated reason. **My guard was wider than
> the fact it guarded ‚Äî the exact defect PX0 exists to fix.**

> ### ‚è≠ 2026-07-14 (20:35 UTC) ‚Äî `origin/main @ bc6cb40b`
>
> ## ‚ñ∂ TWO LANES LIVE: Language = LET-4 ¬∑ Runtime = **PX0** (released, Handoff Gate complete)
>
> **PX0 ‚Äî target-classification erratum** (`wp/px0-target-classification-erratum.md`,
> merged `bc6cb40b`; kickoff `evt_a76fvxyjqrgf`). **Out of band ‚Äî it does NOT wait
> behind CC9.** Re-gate the POSIX boundary from `#[cfg(unix)]` to
> `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`; every other Unix returns a **named unavailable
> lane BEFORE any host call**. **No value changes** ‚Äî *correcting an unprobed
> constant would repeat the defect with a fresher number.* **Why it's urgent:**
> `O_NOFOLLOW_KEN` (`eval.rs:2371`) enforces `SymlinkPolicy::NoFollow` ‚Äî an
> **ADR-0017 confinement property** resting on an unprobed constant under a `cfg`
> that also selects macOS and BSD. **A wrong flag there does not fail; it silently
> does not confine.**
>
> ### ‚úÖ FORK 2 RULED (Architect, `evt_7qqf827rr1jxk`) ‚Äî `rustix`, not our bindings
> **Exact-pinned, checksum-locked `rustix`, private behind a first-party `ken-host`
> policy shell.** I had leaned the other way; **the Architect's counter is better
> than my argument: a probe checks NUMBERS, not SIGNATURES** ‚Äî it cannot validate a
> handwritten signature, calling convention, pointer/length coupling, errno, or
> ownership transfer. **So "keep our bindings + probe the constants" buys the CHEAP
> half of the ABI surface and retains ALL the `unsafe`.** ADR-0009 (*curate before
> construct*) settles the shape. **PX2 survives** ‚Äî the manifest is now an
> **independent cross-check** that fails the build closed on disagreement.
>
> ### ‚òÖ‚òÖ THE ARCHITECT CORRECTED MY CHARTER ON THREE COUNTS. ALL THREE WERE RIGHT.
> **(1) The inventory is ELEVEN constants and SIX FFI declarations ‚Äî I wrote three
> and five.** Off by 3.6√ó, **in a document whose thesis is "the artifact cannot
> state its own contract."** *I indicted the very thing I failed to enumerate.*
> **How:** I read from `:2370` because an audit cited `AT_REMOVEDIR` at `:2375` ‚Äî
> **the inventory begins at `:2355`.** I took my window from *a citation of one
> instance* instead of *the extent of the kind*. **A line number tells you where
> something IS, never where its KIND begins and ends.** And I grepped
> `unsafe extern`, got two hits, **and read one** ‚Äî the second is *indented, inside
> a function body* (`mask_sigpipe()`, `:3720`, carrying `SIGPIPE=13`/`SIG_IGN=1`).
> **(2) Phase-A's exit "no `unsafe` outside `ken-host`" was FALSE and DANGEROUS** ‚Äî
> the JIT `mem::transmute` (`cranelift_backend.rs:1059`) is a **native-execution**
> boundary, not a host-ABI one, and an implementer could have **"satisfied" my AC by
> moving the trampoline into `ken-host`** ‚Äî merging two unrelated trust boundaries
> to green a checkbox. **An AC satisfiable by doing the wrong thing DIRECTS the
> wrong thing, with authority.** **(3) V1 support is `linux`, not `unix`.**
>
> ### üî¥ THREE THINGS OWED BY PAT ‚Äî PX1 does not start without the first
> **(a) DEPENDENCY-RISK ACCEPTANCE:** `rustix` inside the **runtime** trust boundary
> (Sec3 supply chain; `ken-kernel` stays `forbid(unsafe_code)`, untouched). *The
> alternative he'd be declining is not "no unsafe" ‚Äî it's 6 hand-written FFI
> boundaries and 13 unprobed ABI facts.* **(b)** campaign ambition + exit criterion
> (I recommend PX-A‚ÜíPX-C, exit = **the CLI tool as a NATIVE EXECUTABLE**).
> **(c)** native early vs late (**the gap is WIDENING**: +16 ops interp, +0 native).
>

> The report was written at `3a5cd323`; **75 commits have landed since.** Two of

> host ops** (not one), and `Cap` **already carries** a rights bitset, an

> five `unsafe extern "C"` syscalls **inline in the 4,600-line evaluator** with

>
> ## ‚òÖ‚òÖ CC6b was ONE LEMMA from being HOLLOW ‚Äî a VACUOUS law has ZERO trust delta
> The frame's AC4 (`parse (render p) = p` for normalized `p`) was **proved FALSE**
> by Runtime's implementer, who hard-stopped rather than weaken it. **My frame put
> the segment invariant in a `--` COMMENT** (`cc6b-path-posix.md:118`), where the
> carrier `List (List UInt8)` cannot state it. Architect ruled ‚Üí **segment-valid**.
> Shipped: `path_valid` (the invariant **in Ken**), the conditional round-trip, and
> **`path_parse_valid : ‚àÄ raw. path_valid (path_parse raw) ‚â° True` ‚Äî THE REACHING
> LEMMA, PROVED by structural induction, not postulated.**
>
> **runtime-leader argued *"zero trust movement ‚áí no Architect review."* Conclusion
> right, REASON WRONG:** a hollow conditional law **type-checks, adds no `Axiom`,
> and has ZERO `trusted_base()` delta** ‚Äî the gate's trigger **never fires on the
> vacuous WP**, because adding nothing is what it's looking for. **I published
> because I verified the reaching lemma is proved**, not because the delta was
> zero. ‚Üí memory `a-vacuous-law-has-zero-trust-delta`.
>
> ## ‚òÖ‚òÖ The `;` DERIVATION found a spec bug with NOTHING to do with LET-4
> The spec said `match_expr ::= "{" arm (";" arm)* "}"`. **The landed parser accepts
> a trailing `;` before `}`** (`parser.rs:1843-1861`) ‚Äî `match x { P ‚Ü¶ p; }`
> compiles today. It surfaced only because **@architect refused to let the draft's
> *"confirm the `;` is unambiguous"* mean *"check"*.** You cannot derive disjointness
> from a grammar that **misdescribes one of the two contexts**.
> > **The conclusion was TRUE. The spec was WRONG ABOUT WHY** ‚Äî and a derivation
> > from a false premise looks exactly as rigorous as a real one.
> **Ruled IN SCOPE:** fix the spec, **preserve the landed acceptance**.
>
> **‚òÖ THIRD TIME TODAY a defect surfaced ONLY when someone tried to WRITE a
> guarantee and found the vocabulary didn't hold it:** CC6b's invariant in a
> comment ¬∑ LET-1's *readability* with no gate ¬∑ `match`'s grammar. **Stop
> asserting; write the proof; see if your pen has anywhere to land.**
>

>   gates and staircased; not one asked whether the output was readable ‚Äî the

> GROUP with sequential (`let*`) semantics, desugaring to the nested `let`s we
> already have.** No new kernel term, no new keyword, no recursion.
>
> ```ken
> let
>   left_chars  : List Char = string_to_list_char left;
>   right_chars : List Char = string_to_list_char right
> in
>   append Char left_chars right_chars
> ```
>
> **It SUBSUMES LET-1b.** Its S6 canonicalizes a maximal nested chain **into the
> grouped spelling**, so after LET-4 **a nested chain never survives formatting**
> ‚Äî LET-1b's amended ¬ß31 production would be **dead the day LET-4 merged**, and
> it would have put Ergo on `layout.rs` while Language is also on `layout.rs`.

> **‚òÖ The staircase was never a formatting bug. It was a SURFACE bug wearing a
> formatting bug's clothes.** Ken had no way to *say* "these bindings are one
> group," so a group had to be spelled as a nest ‚Äî and we then asked the printer
> to render a nest as though it weren't one. **A workaround fossil: the fix is
> the language, not the printer.** *Pat saw the feature; I was patching the
> symptom.*

> ## ‚úÖ LET-2 **CLOSED** @ `8853b475` (all 3 retros in) ¬∑ ‚úÖ LET-1 CLOSED ¬∑ ‚úÖ SUB-1b CLOSED

> ## ‚õî‚õî THE HEADLINE: **¬ß31 MANDATES A STAIRCASE.** LET-1 was FAITHFUL; the SPEC is wrong.
>
> **LET-1's own frozen oracle asserts this** for a *flat* 3-binding chain ‚Äî each
> RHS forced onto its own line (the binding is **58 cols**; it fits), and each
> `in` indenting its body one more level, so a **sequence** renders as a
> right-nested **staircase**:
>
> ```
> const chars : List Char =
>   let left_chars : List Char =
>     string_to_list_char left
>   in
>     let right_chars : List Char =
>       string_to_list_char right
>     in
>       let joined_chars : List Char =
>         append Char left_chars right_chars
>       in
>         joined_chars      ‚Üê 8 cols right of the binding it belongs to
> ```
>
> **`spec/30-surface/31-lexical.md:216-227` SAYS SO.** Ergo derived it correctly.
> **‚áí Deriving from the contract protects you from laundering your PREFERENCE.
> It does NOT protect you from a DEFECTIVE CONTRACT** ‚Äî and a faithful derivation
> transmits the defect **with full authority**, then freezes it in an exact-text
> oracle, making the bug **evidence for itself**.
>
> **‚òÖ The tell was in the WP's own title.** LET-1 = *"**readable** let-chain
> layout."* Its ACs asserted exact-text ¬∑ AST ¬∑ tokens ¬∑ idempotence ¬∑ ‚â§80 cols ¬∑
> zero-trust. **Six gates, all green, NOT ONE asked whether the output was
> READABLE.** The exact-text oracle *feels* like it closes that gap ‚Äî it doesn't;
> its expected value came from the same defective production, so **it agrees with
> the defect by construction.** *We replaced a stability gate with a different
> stability gate and called it a quality gate.* ‚Üí fleet memory
> `deriving-from-the-contract-cannot-detect-a-defective-contract.md`.
>

> ## ‚ñ∂ CC6b ‚Äî hard-stopped and RULED. Runtime building.
> **runtime-implementer proved AC4 FALSE against the carrier I mandated.** `Path
> = MkPath Bool (List (List UInt8))` cannot express *"each segment is nonempty
> and contains no `/`"* ‚Äî **my frame put that invariant in a `--` COMMENT**

>
> CV reported all 3 `catalog/guide/**` strands failing `ParseError: found
> Ident("program")`. **I built a confident mechanism** (lexer keywordizes
> `program` unconditionally ‚Üí an `Ident` token means the parser was mid-expression
> ‚Üí an unterminated declaration upstream ‚Üí *"the header is the crime scene, not the
> crime"*).
>

> pre-LET-2 content.** ‚áí **The observation may never have been real.** And

> touched by the fix**, so it cannot have been repaired. And spec-author's real
> repair (`fn bool_eq_sound` ‚Üí `lemma`, an Œ©-valued decl wrongly declared `fn`) is
> an **ELABORATION** error that **cannot** produce a **ParseError**. *Two faults,
> welded into one story by me.*
>
> **‚áí THE GREPS WERE ALL TRUE AND THE CONCLUSION WAS UNSUPPORTED. I should have
> questioned the OBSERVATION, not explained it.** *When a report and a mechanism
> disagree, verify the report is real before building a mechanism that reconciles
> them.*
>
> **‚õî BLOCKING LET-2's AC1 ‚Äî the one question:** does
> `catalog/guide/decomposition-abstraction.ken.md` parse+elaborate on current main,
> **unmodified**? **YES** ‚áí artifact, my mechanism withdrawn, AC1 correctly scoped
> to the 2 repaired strands. **NO** ‚áí **LET-2 would merge with AC1 UNMET on a strand
> nobody fixed.** *Do not infer it from the aggregate green.*

>
> **Pat:** *"We currently underuse `let` in ken code, and suffer readability loss
> due to it‚Ä¶ prepare a few WPs: one for the doc/agent guidance, one for kenfmt
> changes, and one for the catalog rewrites."* Report:
> `local/ken-let-authoring-style-report.md` (sol agent, 2026-07-14). **I verified
